meta.name: Inked Top-Down Medieval Battlemap Style
meta.medium: SVG, procedurally generated, hand-inked illustration look
meta.one_line: True overhead battlemap art where every form carries a wobbly dark ink outline and is built from many individually-drawn, weathered, jittered pieces (tiles, stone blocks, planks, moss, foliage lobes), lit by a single upper-right sun casting soft shadows to the lower-left.
meta.intended_use: Author a consistent collection of overhead map assets (ground tiles, roads/paths, buildings, fountains, wells, market stalls, dungeon rooms with doors, forests, props) that composite together on one grid map.
distinguishing_traits: visible hand-drawn INK OUTLINES on every shape
surfaces built from hundreds of small individually-inked pieces, not smooth gradient fills
heavy deliberate weathering: moss, broken tiles, loose planks, stains, cracks, rust
single directional light (upper-right) with real cast shadows to the lower-left
reproducible via a fixed random seed per asset

shared_constants.note: Fix these ONCE for the whole collection so every asset agrees. Only the seed changes per asset.
shared_constants.shadow_offset_note: scale magnitude by object height; taller objects throw longer, softer shadows
shared_constants.seed: integer, one fixed value documented per asset (e.g. building=7, fountain=14, stall=23, dungeon=31, forest=45)

projection_and_camera.view: true top-down / bird's-eye, orthographic; no perspective, no visible vertical walls
projection_and_camera.height_cue: objects read as height ONLY through their lower-left cast shadow and a thin lower-left ambient-occlusion (AO) rim; the up-right edge catches a light rim
projection_and_camera.grid: designed for a square battlemap grid; hold one pixels_per_square for the whole collection so buildings, stalls and props share scale
projection_and_camera.tiling: ground assets (cobble, grass, plaza, road, forest floor) tile seamlessly at ground_cell_pitch; corridor/road stubs should run to the tile edge to connect neighbours

rendering_technique.core: ink-first illustration: draw a filled shape, then ALWAYS give it a dark outline; complex surfaces are a dense field of small individually-outlined pieces
rendering_technique.hand_drawn_wobble: no straight machine edges. Every rectangle's 4 corners are individually jittered by +/-jitter_amplitude so lines look drawn by hand; organic shapes use a jittered smoothed polygon (blob)
rendering_technique.procedural_fill: surfaces (roofs, walls, floors, cobbles, canopies) are generated by looping a piece across the region with per-piece color, size and offset randomness
rendering_technique.reproducibility: seed the RNG once per asset so results are deterministic and re-editable
rendering_technique.typical_piece_count: 300 (single prop) to ~5500 (a dense forest); batch pieces by material and keep blur filters only on shadow layers
not_this: do NOT use smooth vector gradients as the PRIMARY surface (only as overlays)
do NOT leave any shape outline-less
do NOT use a perfectly regular grid without jitter
do NOT make anything look brand-new and undamaged

primitives.note: Four core helpers underpin every asset. Pseudocode given; port to any language that emits SVG paths.
primitives.jitter(v, a=0.9): return v + random_uniform(-a, a)   // the hand-drawn wobble amount
primitives.wrect(x,y,w,h, j=0.9, rnd=1.6): a rectangle whose 4 corners are each jittered by +/-j, then emitted as a path with slightly rounded (quadratic-bezier) corners of radius rnd -> a wobbly hand-drawn rounded rect. Used for tiles, stone blocks, planks, doors, crates.
primitives.blob(cx,cy,r, n=8, amp=0.34): closed path of n points at radius r*(1 +/- amp) around (cx,cy), smoothed with quadratic beziers through the midpoints -> an organic shape. Used for moss, cobbles, canopy lobes, water, sacks, rocks, dirt patches.
primitives.P(d, fill, ink='#312619', w=0.9, op=1.0): emit ONE <path> carrying BOTH fill and stroke so every shape is inked in a single call; ink=null means no stroke (used only for soft overlays/shadows).
primitives.catmull(control_points, samples_per_seg=: Catmull-Rom spline sampler for smooth winding centrelines (roads, paths, rivers).

linework.role: the signature of the style; present on every element
linework.linejoin: round; endpoints slightly jittered; vary weight subtly between pieces to avoid uniformity

light_and_shadow.sun: single hard-ish sun from the UPPER-RIGHT (~45deg)
light_and_shadow.cast_shadow: each raised object drops a shadow to the LOWER-LEFT: offset approx (-16,+16)px scaled by height, fill a dark tone at opacity ~0.26-0.33, blurred (gaussian stdDeviation ~5-6)
light_and_shadow.prop_shadows: chimneys, planks, posts, barrels, trees each cast their own small lower-left shadow (darker tone ~0.28, blur ~2.2)
light_and_shadow.interior_ao: thin darkening along the lower-left INNER edges of a form; for rooms, a soft gradient onto the floor along the lower-left walls
light_and_shadow.up_right_rim: a faint warm light rim along the up-right outer edge of raised forms
light_and_shadow.global_light_overlay: over each surface/asset lay a linear gradient from warm light at the upper-right (#fff4d8 @0.10) through transparent mid to dark at the lower-left (#1a1408 @0.16-0.20) to unify the sun
light_and_shadow.dappled_light: for open ground under a canopy or along a path, scatter soft warm radial glints (#fff4d8) biased upper-right
light_and_shadow.consistency_rule: EVERY asset uses this exact light direction so shadows and highlights agree on the assembled map

color.philosophy: earthy, weathered, mid-saturation; reds and slate-blues for roofs, warm greys for stone and cobble, olive-greens for moss and foliage, tan for wood, teal for water, dark warm neutrals for iron and void
color.value_rule: keep a wide value range within each surface via the piece-color pools so texture reads; avoid flat single-color fills

gradients_and_filters.light_overlay: linearGradient x1=1 y1=0 -> x2=0 y2=1 : light_warm@0.10 -> transparent -> light_dark@0.16
gradients_and_filters.slope_shading: linearGradient darkening toward eave/lower-left, transparent-to-light toward ridge/upper-right (for pitched roofs)
gradients_and_filters.inner_ao: linearGradient dark@~0.30 fading to transparent, laid along the lower-left inner edges of rooms/basins
gradients_and_filters.water_grad: radialGradient offset to upper-right: light center -> transparent -> dark rim (darker toward lower-left)
gradients_and_filters.soft_shadow: filter feGaussianBlur stdDeviation ~5-6, applied to large cast-shadow paths only
gradients_and_filters.soft_prop_shadow: filter feGaussianBlur stdDeviation ~2.2-2.4 for small prop shadows
gradients_and_filters.performance_rule: keep filters ONLY on shadow layers; everything else is crisp inked paths

roof_tiles.build: fill each roof slope with staggered courses of small jittered rounded-rects (wrect); a ridge splits two slopes
roof_tiles.color: weighted pick from roof_red_pool OR roof_slate_pool; 5% tile_moss, 5% tile_broken_dark
roof_tiles.shading: gradient overlay darkening toward eaves + lower-left; ridge gets a light highlight line above and a dark line below
roof_tiles.variants: terracotta (red pool) or slate (blue-grey pool); mix building-to-building for map variety

masonry_stone.build: base grey rect, then fill with running-bond courses of jittered block-rects (half-block offset per alternating row)
masonry_stone.color: stone_pool over stone_wall_base; mortar reads via ink outlines (and a dark base showing through gaps)
masonry_stone.usage: wall borders (~20-30px thick), ridge spines, chimneys, fountain basins, wells, plinths, bridge/stair edges, garden walls, dungeon walls
masonry_stone.ring_variant: for circular basins/wells, place blocks around a ring between an inner and outer radius, one wedge per block, tangent to the circle
masonry_stone.opening_variant: to cut a doorway/gate, skip any block whose centre falls inside a defined opening rect
masonry_stone.shading: light edge on the up-right side, dark edge on the down-left side

flagstone_floor.build: dark mortar base rect, then big jittered square slabs (wrect ~pitch-5) on a grid, optional half-slab row offset, only where inside the room/corridor mask
flagstone_floor.detail: cracks (thin jittered dark polylines), worn traffic path (low-opacity dark blob-stroke), stains, puddles, moss in shaded corners, rubble bits

timber_wood.build: long jittered rounded-rect + ~3 interior grain lines + a small lower-left offset shadow, all ink-outlined
timber_wood.usage: loose planks, ridge beams, doors, stall posts and counters, fences, carts, well beams, crates/barrels, fallen logs
timber_wood.color: wood_pool (or wood_pool_dark for dungeon) with wood_grain lines; log end-caps get a ring ellipse

moss_ivy.build: a dark green jittered blob (moss_base, ink moss_ink) speckled with many small lighter-green circles from moss_speckle_pool
moss_ivy.placement: along ridges, roof valleys, wall bases, shaded corners, stone rings, and as trailing vines; DENSER on the lower-left/shaded side

cobblestone_ground.build: jittered grid of ~7-sided blobs packed edge-to-edge, alternate rows offset, light ink so ground recedes
cobblestone_ground.detail: grass tufts (3-5 blades) in the gaps; worn ruts/darker lanes for roads; occasional puddle or dropped plank

grass_dirt.build: grass_base fill + many low-opacity grass_pool blob patches + blade tufts (3-5 short curved lines fanning up); worn dirt = dirt_pool blob patches
grass_dirt.usage: yards, forest floor, clearings, edges, planters

path_road.build: stroke a smooth winding centreline (catmull) with a WIDE round-cap stroke in path_base; overlay a narrower path_light stroke for a worn centre; add a central rut plus two offset ruts (path_rut)

params.edge_break: scatter dirt_pool blobs straddling the edge so it is organic

path_road.detail: pebbles, grass-in-cracks, puddles, litter, dappled light; run the path off two tile edges to connect neighbours
path_road.note: on a forest/greenery tile, EXCLUDE canopy pieces from the path corridor so it stays open

water.build: layered blue blob fills (water_pool) inside an inked stone rim; a radial light gradient (lit upper-right, dark lower-left rim); thin lighter ripple arcs; a few water_highlight glints; wet darkening toward the rim
water.usage: fountain pools, wells, troughs, puddles, streams
water.detail: wet darkening on adjacent stone; optional foam ring at a spout

cloth_canvas.build: quads/stripes of canvas_awning_pool, ink-outlined, with a sagging/scalloped free edge and a soft underside shadow
cloth_canvas.usage: market-stall awnings, banners, tent roofs, umbrellas
cloth_canvas.shading: lit crease line near the top, gradient darkening toward the sagging front edge

foliage_canopy.build: top-down tree = an ink-outlined dark base blob (silhouette), then a bumpy field of mid-green lobe-blobs, then lighter speckle-blobs biased to the UPPER-RIGHT, then tiny bright highlight dots on the sunny side, then a lower-left dark shaded crescent inside the canopy
foliage_canopy.color: foliage_dark base, foliage_mid_pool lobes, foliage_light_pool speckles, foliage_highlight dots, foliage_shade crescent; foliage_ink outlines
foliage_canopy.detail: optional small dark trunk-centre dot on ~30%; bushes are the same recipe, smaller and lower; match moss greens so foliage and overgrowth read as one world
foliage_canopy.density: for a forest, place trees on a jittered grid (pitch ~52) and draw back-to-front (sorted by y); draw ALL soft shadows first as one layer, then all canopies

iron_metal.build: dark rounded-rects (iron_pool) with an iron_ink outline, an iron_highlight rim on the up-right edge, and stud dots; add rust speckles
iron_metal.usage: door bands and studs, ring handles, portcullis bars, chains, brackets

weathering_and_damage.principle: nothing is pristine; every surface shows age
techniques: 5% of roof tiles broken-dark or missing (show underlayer/gap)
5-10% of tiles/stones moss-covered, concentrated on shaded edges
irregular low-opacity stain blotches on roofs, walls and floors
loose planks and debris dropped on roofs and ground
cracked/warped courses: let occasional rows sag or blocks tilt (extra jitter)
vine trails and moss creeping across large flat surfaces and up shaded wall bases
worn ruts and grass-in-cracks on cobble and dirt paths
rust speckles on iron; leaf litter and fallen logs in forests

layer_stack.name: ground_or_void
layer_stack.content: cobble/grass/flagstone base for the tile (or dark void outside dungeon walls)

layer_stack.name: ground_detail
layer_stack.content: path surface, dirt patches, tufts, litter, worn lanes, dappled light

layer_stack.name: cast_shadows
layer_stack.content: soft lower-left shadows of all raised objects, drawn on the ground

layer_stack.name: structure_base
layer_stack.content: stone footprints, basin/well rings, stall floors, wall rings, plinths (masonry, ink-outlined)

layer_stack.name: surface_fields
layer_stack.content: roof tile fields, water surfaces, canvas awnings, plank decks, floor slabs

layer_stack.name: spines_and_frames
layer_stack.content: ridge spines, beams, stall posts, chimney bodies, door frames

layer_stack.name: surface_shading
layer_stack.content: slope gradients, valley/eave darkening, ridge highlight, inner AO, global light overlay

layer_stack.name: overgrowth
layer_stack.content: moss/ivy clumps, vine trails, foliage canopies

layer_stack.name: props_and_debris
layer_stack.content: loose planks, crates, barrels, sacks, goods, buckets, bones, chains; each with its own small shadow

layer_stack.name: silhouette_ink
layer_stack.content: bold ink outline around the whole structure to seat it on the map

procedure_for_new_asset: 1. Set shared constants: light vector, ink colors, pitches, pixels_per_square, jitter amp, and a fresh RNG seed.
2. Lay the ground (cobble/grass/flagstone) or the dark void for the asset's tile.
3. Add ground detail (paths, dirt, tufts, dappled light).
4. Define the footprint; drop its cast shadow to the lower-left FIRST so it sits under the object.
5. Build the base in masonry/timber (walls, basins, decks, posts, rings) as inked jittered pieces.
6. Fill surfaces from the right material_system (roof_tiles / water / canvas / planks / flagstone / canopy), looping pieces with per-piece randomness.
7. Add spines/frames (ridge, beams, chimney, door frames) then the shading pass (slope/AO gradients + global light overlay).
8. Weather it: moss/ivy on shaded edges, broken/missing pieces, stains, cracks, vine trails, rust.
9. Scatter props/debris, each with its own small lower-left shadow.
10. Stroke the bold silhouette ink around the whole structure.
11. Verify it tiles/aligns with neighbours at the shared pitch and that its shadow agrees with the shared sun.

asset_recipes.ground_tile: cobblestone_ground OR grass_dirt across the tile; tufts/weeds in cracks; must tile at cell_pitch; no cast shadow (it is ground)
asset_recipes.road_path: path_road over a ground tile; worn central ruts; edging stones optional; run off two edges to connect; keep cobble/dirt ink light so structures read above
asset_recipes.building: masonry wall border (~20px) footprint; fill interior with roof_tiles split by a ridge spine; chimney(s) straddling the ridge; moss on ridge/valleys/corners; loose planks; slope shading + light overlay; lower-left cast shadow; optional plank door + window darks in a revealed wall strip; bold silhouette ink. Variants: red vs slate roof, L/cross plan with two ridges, attached lean-to, size and number of chimneys.
asset_recipes.fountain: masonry ring basin (circular block ring); layered water inside (ripple arcs, glints, dark lower-left rim); raised central plinth casting a small shadow onto the water; optional upper tier/basin and central spout with a dashed foam ring; moss on the shaded stone; lower-left cast shadow. Variants: tiered (concentric rings), statue plinth, rectangular trough.
asset_recipes.well: small masonry ring + two timber posts and a cross-beam + rope and wooden bucket; dark water disk inside; moss at base; lower-left shadow
asset_recipes.market_stall: small footprint; four timber corner posts (each a tiny shadow); striped cloth_canvas awning over the back with a sagging/scalloped front edge + underside shadow; plank counter in front holding goods (produce = small bright blobs, sacks = tan blobs, crates/barrels = short timber); side props; lower-left cast shadow of the awning
asset_recipes.wall_fence: run of masonry_stone OR timber posts+rails; moss at base; thin lower-left shadow along its length; break/ruin sections for age
asset_recipes.dungeon_room: dark void outside; flagstone_floor inside a thick masonry wall ring (running bond); cut door OPENINGS by skipping blocks in opening rects; run corridor floor stubs through openings to the tile edge; inner AO gradients along lower-left inner walls; DOORS: banded plank door (timber + iron bands + studs + ring handle), open door leaf swung into a corridor, and/or iron portcullis (vertical bars over darkness, rust + moss); scatter debris (barrels, broken crate, bones, chain, rubble); bold silhouette ink
asset_recipes.tree_bush: foliage_canopy recipe; round lower-left cast shadow; bushes are smaller/lower; match moss greens so foliage and overgrowth read as one world
asset_recipes.forest: grass_dirt floor + litter + dappled light; many overlapping foliage canopies on a jittered grid drawn back-to-front (all shadows first, then all canopies); optional glade kept tree-free holding understory (fallen log, rocks, ferns, mushrooms); optional wide winding path (path_road) with canopies excluded from its corridor
asset_recipes.props_debris: crates/barrels (short stout timber + lid/hoop), sacks (tan blobs with a cinched top), hay, a cart (plank bed + two wheel circles), stacked planks, bones (bone-colored blob skull + long bones), chains (linked iron ellipses); each ink-outlined with its own small lower-left shadow; scatter a few to tie the map together
asset_recipes.stairs_platform: stack of masonry slabs, each step edge a dark lower-left shadow line so the flight reads as descending toward the light-facing side

collection_consistency.shared_light: one sun direction (upper-right) for every asset; identical shadow offset/opacity/blur
collection_consistency.shared_ink: ink_default and the material inks identical across assets; keep the same weight ranges
collection_consistency.shared_scale: fix pixels_per_square once and size buildings/props/stalls/rooms to it
collection_consistency.shared_ground_pitch: cobble/grass cell_pitch and flagstone pitch identical everywhere so ground pieces tile and roads meet plazas
collection_consistency.shared_jitter: use the same jitter amplitude so the 'drawing hand' feels the same on every piece
collection_consistency.shared_palette: draw all colors from palette_tokens; introduce new hues only via a new material_system, never ad hoc
collection_consistency.seeding: give each asset its own fixed seed and document it so any asset can be regenerated identically

do: build surfaces from many small jittered, individually-colored pieces
keep one upper-right light and lower-left shadows for the whole collection
weather everything: moss, breaks, stains, loose planks, cracks, rust
draw ground cobbles/floors with lighter ink so structures read above them
seed the RNG and reuse shared palette/scale/jitter across assets
run roads/corridors off tile edges so assets connect
dont: use smooth gradient fills as the main surface (only as overlays)
leave any shape without an outline
use perfectly straight, unjittered edges or a dead-regular grid
let different assets use different light directions or ink weights
introduce off-palette colors except through a defined material_system
make anything look brand-new and undamaged
